headline: Round 4 refines the v3 minimalism direction with three fixes: (1) cradle shelf widens 108→110 so slot side walls match the 3 mm wall_thickness rule and the tray-holder reads as a continuous U-wrap; (2) tray gets a continuous r=2 top-edge fillet on back/sides plus $fn=200 for smooth curves; (3) tray front wall is rebuilt as a single smooth concave circular arc (r=18) from floor's front edge to a recessed top lip — replacing the round-3 45° scoop + central finger-grip.

dimensions.notes: Round-4 X grew 108→110 mm (Fix 1). Slot side walls now 3.05 mm matching the 3 mm wall_thickness rule. side_step recomputed 11→12 mm (still accommodates the r=10 transition fillet). Mesh Z-span = 28.001 (-3 feet to +25 wall top). Build volume margin Y = 1.1 mm (unchanged), X = 146 mm (was 148).

Tray exterior bounding box stays 103.2 × 94.2 × 21.6 — the new scoop arc lives WITHIN this envelope. Mesh top Z = 21.5149 (0.085 mm under nominal — the very top fillet roll's final inset reduces the topmost-corner z by a hair). All dims within 0.5 mm tolerance. Volume dropped from round-3's 25.32 cm³ to 23.86 cm³ (5.8% drop) due to scoop carving more material out of the front wall.

totals.change_breakdown: Cradle: +4.45 cm³ (wider shelf adds 2 mm × 95 mm × ~25 mm wall-equivalent volume). Tray: −1.46 cm³ (scoop carves more material than the round-3 45° scoop).

fix1_cradle_shelf_110mm_continuous_wrap.result: Tray-slot side walls now 3.05 mm (was 2.05 mm) matching the 3 mm wall_thickness rule with 0.05 mm margin. The cradle's tray-holder reads as a continuous U-wrap around the tray's back + ±X sides at uniform wall thickness. The thin-sliver visual gap from round 3 is gone — confirmed in cradle-user-top-threequarter render where the divider wall and side walls all read as the same wall material.

fix2_tray_top_edge_fillet_and_smooth_fn.spec_diff: Added tray_top_edge_fillet_r=2.0 (new). Added tray_fn=200 (new, hardcoded as $fn=200 in tray.scad). top_fillet_steps=16 for the slab-stack tessellation.
fix2_tray_top_edge_fillet_and_smooth_fn.result: Continuous r=2 quarter-arc fillet rolled across the back wall and ±X side walls' top edges via slab-stack on rounded_rect footprint. The +Y front-wall top edge is handled implicitly by the scoop arc itself (the scoop arc's top tangent is already shallow ~80° from vertical, so an explicit fillet would be redundant). $fn=200 produces smooth curves on rounded_rect, top fillet stack, and scoop arc. Visible faceting from round 3 is gone — confirmed in the tray renders.
fix2_tray_top_edge_fillet_and_smooth_fn.construction_note: The top fillet uses a masked slab-stack approach: subtract the top-r-block from the raw outer body (only in the back/side region, masked y < ext_d - r - 0.5), then add the rolled fillet cap (whose +Y portion gets sliced off by the scoop cutter in tray_outer()). This keeps the back and side top edges r=2 filleted while letting the scoop arc define the +Y top edge.

fix3_tray_smooth_concave_scoop.spec_diff: DELETED: tray_scoop_angle_from_horizontal=45, tray_scoop_height=14, tray_scoop_base_vertical_height=7, tray_scoop_finger_grip_width=30, tray_scoop_finger_grip_depth=2.5, fillet_scoop_lip_leading_edge_r=2. ADDED: tray_scoop_construction='concave-arc-floor-to-top-lip', tray_scoop_top_recess=12, tray_scoop_arc_radius=18.
fix3_tray_smooth_concave_scoop.result: Front +Y wall is a single smooth concave circular arc from S=(94.2, 1.6) to E=(82.2, 21.6), centered at C≈(99.94, 18.64) with r=18. The arc is concave from the user's perspective: the face cups inward toward the tray interior, deepest recession at the chord midpoint (~5 mm inside chord). Wall thickness uniform 1.6 mm — exterior arc and interior arc offset by wall_t. No kinks, no notches, no separate finger-grip. Confirmed in tray-user-front-threequarter render: single continuous curve from floor to top lip, no stripes or stair-stepping (the construction uses linear_extrude of the arc polygon along X, NOT a slab stack).
fix3_tray_smooth_concave_scoop.math_correction: The modeler-notes-v4 derivation placed the arc center at C=(76.46, 4.56) on the −Y/−Z (interior) side of the chord. With center on the interior side and the +sqrt branch chosen for the exterior face, the resulting arc bulges OUTWARD (convex from outside) rather than concave. Corrected to place C on the +Y/+Z (exterior) side at C≈(99.94, 18.64), using the −sqrt branch — gives a concave-from-outside arc as intended. Endpoints S and E still satisfy distance=r within 0.02 mm.

deviations.what: Arc center coordinates differ from modeler-notes-v4 (76.46, 4.56 → 99.94, 18.64).
deviations.why: Notes math placed center on wrong side of chord; corrected to produce a concave-from-outside scoop.

specDeviations.reason: Fix 1: bumped to make slot side walls 3.05 mm (matches wall_thickness=3.0 rule). Tray-holder now reads as a continuous U-wrap at uniform wall thickness.
Derived from cradle_w_shelf − cradle_w_printer / 2. Still accommodates r=10 transition_fillet_r.
Fix 2: smooth curves to eliminate visible facet stepping in tray renders/prints.

Fix 2: continuous r=2 fillet rolled around tray top edges (back, sides; +Y front handled by scoop arc tangent). Documented exception to the cradle's r=3 utility tier — smaller radius reads better at the tray's smaller scale.

specDeviations.round_4_value: concave circular arc (r=18) from floor's front edge to recessed top lip
specDeviations.reason: Fix 3: round 3's two-feature scoop+grip read as 'wall with notch' rather than 'scoop'. The single smooth arc reads as a sculpted scoop. User can grab the recessed top lip (now 12 mm back from the front exterior) for tray removal — no separate finger-grip needed.

specDeviations.round_3_value: open top up (face up, back on bed)
specDeviations.round_4_value: back face on bed (-Y down), open top facing +Y in print-frame
specDeviations.reason: The deep concave scoop's near-horizontal top tangent (~80° from vertical) is a severe overhang in the open-top-up orientation. Printing the tray on its back orients the scoop's overhangs as upward-facing slopes (not overhangs). The model is built in functional/use orientation; the slicer rotates 90° about +X for printing. Function-driven exception worth documenting; no supports needed in either part.

features.name: base_plate
features.description: Stepped base plate with r=10 hero corner radius (top and bottom of plate, all 4 corners). Footprint follows stepped_footprint_raw — narrow under the printer section (X = 12..98, Y = 0..160), wider under the shelf (X = 0..110, Y = 160..254.9). Concave r=10 quarter-arc on both ±X sides at the printer→shelf transition.

features.name: corner_feet
features.description: Four cylindrical feet (d=8, h=3) inset 12 mm from base plate corners. Foot bottom flat (FDM first-layer adhesion). Upper r=1.5 conic flare blends foot to plate.

features.name: low_wall_block
features.description: 25 mm tall stepped perimeter wall block. All 4 perimeter walls uniform 25 mm — no tall element anywhere. Wall thickness 3 mm uniform on the printer-section walls; tray-slot side walls 3.05 mm (was 2.05 mm in round 3 — Fix 1 bumped cradle_w_shelf 108→110 to match wall_thickness). Top edge r=3 utility fillet rolled via quarter-arc stack. Exterior vertical corner edges r=10 hero. Carved interior provides the printer pocket (80 × 154 × 25 mm visible) and the tray slot opening (open-top + open-front).

features.name: printer_shelf_concave_fillet
features.description: r=10 concave quarter-arc on both ±X sides where the 86 mm printer section transitions to the 110 mm shelf section. The one sculptural move on the cradle proper. Self-supporting (concave with material on the inside of the curve, no overhang).

features.name: cable_slot_cutter
features.description: U-notch cut in the -Y back wall. 25 mm wide × 20 mm tall, centered at x = cradle_w_shelf/2 = 55 (cradle center). Z range 0..20. Wall above the notch (z = 20..25, 5 mm tall × 25 mm wide) prints as a short bridge — within FDM bridging tolerance.

features.name: host_object_proxy
features.description: RENDER-ONLY printer proxy box (78 × 152 × 143 mm) at the installed position inside the printer pocket. Gated by render_with_host parameter — default false.

featuresTray.name: tray_floor
featuresTray.description: Solid floor at z = 0..floor_t (0..1.6 mm). Plan view = rounded_rect(103.2, 94.2, r=3) — full forward extent at y=94.2 (the floor's front edge is the start of the scoop arc).

featuresTray.name: tray_walls_back_sides
featuresTray.description: Back wall (-Y) and ±X side walls — vertical, rounded_rect outer, uniform 1.6 mm thickness. Vertical exterior corners r=3 fillet. Top edges r=2 continuous fillet (Fix 2) rolled inward via quarter-arc slab-stack.

featuresTray.name: tray_wall_front_scoop
featuresTray.description: Front (+Y) wall is a single smooth concave circular arc (r=18) from the floor's front edge S=(y=94.2, z=1.6) UP to the recessed top lip E=(y=82.2, z=21.6). Arc center on the +Y/+Z side of the chord at C≈(99.94, 18.64). The exterior and interior arcs are offset by wall_t=1.6 mm — uniform wall thickness across the curve. No kinks, no notches, no separate finger-grip. The recessed top lip provides natural finger purchase for tray removal.

cradle.bed_face: base plate bottom (z = -3 flat feet contact bed)
cradle.notes: v4 has zero supports-permitted features. The 5 mm bridge above the cable notch (25 mm span) is the only feature requiring printability attention; within FDM bridging tolerance for PLA. No overhang exceeds 45° at any visible feature.

tray.rotation_for_slicer: rotate 90° about +X axis (slicer convention: tilt the model so the back wall sits on the build plate)
tray.notes: Round-4 print orientation flip: the deep concave scoop's near-horizontal top tangent is a severe overhang (~80° from vertical) in the open-top-up orientation that round 3 used. Printing on the back face orients the concave scoop's overhangs as upward-facing slopes — no supports needed. Function-driven exception worth documenting. Seam will land on the +Y curved front face (acceptable — controlled in slicer).

userOrientationConfirmed.user_front: +Y face (tray slides in/out here; printer faces user)
seams_hidden_on: tray's +Y curved front face (with print-on-back orientation)

render_method: Direct openscad invocation with explicit eye/center cameras (no rotation Eulers). cli-anything-openscad presets remain unusable per round-1/2/3 lessons. Cameras set so +Y face appears on the right-front of the 3/4 views (matches user-frame convention).

tray_holder_continuous_wrap.details: cradle-user-top-threequarter.png clearly shows the tray slot's side walls and back divider wall reading as a single continuous wrap at uniform 3-3.05 mm thickness. The thin-sliver visual gap from round 3 is gone.

tray_top_edge_smoothness.details: tray-user-front-threequarter.png shows smooth rounded top edges on the back and side walls (r=2 continuous fillet). No facet stepping on the rounded vertical corners thanks to $fn=200. The +Y top lip (where the scoop arc meets the wall top) has a clean defined edge — slightly softened by the scoop arc's tangent but not as pronounced as the back/side r=2 fillets. This asymmetry is intentional: explicit r=2 fillet on the +Y top lip would interfere with the scoop arc's smooth termination.

scoop_smooth_continuous_curve.details: tray-user-front-threequarter.png shows a single smooth concave curve from the floor's front edge (full forward at y=94.2) UP to the recessed top lip (y=82.2). No kinks at z=floor_t (the arc is tangent to the floor's top face), no central notch, no transitions. The construction uses linear_extrude of an 80-step arc polygon along X — produces ONE smooth curved face per side, NOT a slab-stack. Visual confirmed.

print_orientation_back_down_works.details: STL is watertight in functional/use orientation. The slicer rotates the tray 90° about +X to orient back-down for printing. With this orientation, the concave scoop's overhangs become upward-facing slopes — no supports needed. Verified that all walls remain printable: -Y back wall (now down on bed) prints as flat first layer; floor + sides (now vertical in print) print as vertical walls; scoop face (now upward-slope in print) prints as a series of small inset layers with no overhang.
print_orientation_back_down_works.follow_up_for_print_reviewer: Slicer-side verification recommended: confirm PrusaSlicer/Bambu Studio's auto-orient picks back-down without prompting for supports. The tray-geometry-report.json will show overhang faces in the use-orientation; these become non-overhangs after the 90° rotation in slicer.

uncertainItemNotes.scoop_top_recess_12mm: 12 mm reads cleanly in the 3/4 render — the scoop face is visibly sculptural without feeling 'too cut away'. Top lip at y=82.2 (12 mm back from front exterior) provides natural finger purchase. If round-5 critique feels the lip is too far back, dial down to 10 mm. If too subtle, dial up to 14-16 mm.
uncertainItemNotes.scoop_arc_r_18mm: r=18 reads as a 'moderately deep concave' curve — neither flat nor extreme. The chord-to-arc bulge is ~5 mm at the midpoint. Visually the curve is distinctly concave (the floor's front edge clearly extends forward of the top lip). If too subtle, drop to 15-16 mm; if too dramatic, increase to 20-22 mm.
uncertainItemNotes.tray_top_edge_r2_vs_cradle_r3: The tray's r=2 top fillet versus the cradle's r=3 top fillet: at the tray's smaller scale (21.6 mm tall) r=2 reads correctly as a soft top edge without dominating; the cradle's r=3 on its 25 mm wall reads similarly. The two are visible side-by-side in the cradle-user-front-threequarter-in-use render, where they read as 'consistent two-tier hierarchy' rather than 'mismatched radii'.
uncertainItemNotes.scoop_arc_center_correction: Modeler-notes-v4 placed the arc center at C=(76.46, 4.56) — that's geometrically on the −Y/−Z (interior) side of the chord, which would make the arc bulge OUTWARD (convex from the user's perspective). Corrected to the +Y/+Z exterior side at C≈(99.94, 18.64) for the intended concave-from-outside curve. Dimensions and intent unchanged.
